DetectionRule.Name: Ransomware Rapid File Encryption
DetectionRule.Description: Detects rapid encryption of multiple files by a single process, characteristic of ransomware payload execution. Uses statistical analysis of file modification patterns and entropy changes.
DetectionRule.DetectionLogic:
let EncryptionThreshold = 10; let TimeWindow = 5m; let MinFileSize = 1024; // 1KB minimum
DeviceFileEvents | where ActionType == "FileCreated" or ActionType == "FileModified" | where Timestamp > ago(TimeWindow) | where FileSize > MinFileSize | extend Extension = tostring(split(FileName, '.')[-1]) | where Extension in~ ("encrypted", "locked", "crypto", "enc", "crypt") or PreviousFileName !~ FileName | summarize FilesAffected = dcount(SHA256), UniqueExtensions = makeset(Extension), TotalSize = sum(FileSize), Processes = makeset(InitiatingProcessFileName) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath | where FilesAffected >= EncryptionThreshold | extend AlertScore = case( FilesAffected >= 50, "Critical", FilesAffected >= 20, "High", FilesAffected >= 10, "Medium", "Low" ) | project-reorder Timestamp, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName, FilesAffected, TotalSize, AlertScore

SuppressionRules.SuppressionDuration:
PT1H
BackupService* EncryptionService*
Windows Defender* msedg*

RemediationSteps.Step: Isolate affected device immediately via Defender XDR
Block initiating process hash across all devices
Identify patient zero through network connections
Preserve forensic evidence (memory dump, disk image)
Check for lateral movement to other systems
Initiate incident response playbook for ransomware

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude legitimate encryption tools with approved certificates
Require minimum 10 files affected in 5 minutes
Analyze process lineage for known-good parents

DetectionRule.Name: Shadow Copy Deletion for Ransomware
DetectionRule.Description: Detects deletion or modification of Volume Shadow Copies, a common precursor to ransomware encryption to prevent file recovery. Monitors vssadmin, wmic, and PowerShell shadow copy manipulation.

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Isolate affected device and disconnect from network
Preserve existing shadow copies if not already deleted
Check for ransomware encryption activity
Review event logs for initial access vector
Restore shadow copy functionality after threat removal

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude legitimate backup operations during maintenance windows
Exclude scheduled maintenance scripts by hash
Time-based: Alert only outside approved backup windows

DetectionRule.Name: Ransomware Suspicious Process Tree
DetectionRule.Description: Detects abnormal process creation patterns characteristic of ransomware execution chains, including spawned cmd.exe/powershell.exe from Office processes, suspicious script execution, and lateral movement tools.

RemediationSteps.Step: IMMEDIATE: Kill suspicious processes and isolate device
Block process hashes and parent-child combinations globally
Check for lateral movement via PsExec, WMI, or scheduled tasks
Analyze network connections for C2 communication
Preserve memory and disk for forensic analysis
Hunt for additional compromised systems

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude known administrative tools and scripts
Time-based analysis: Alert off-hours only for non-IT staff
Risk-based: Require minimum risk score threshold
Process lineage: Validate legitimate administration chains

DetectionRule.Name: Network Share Mass File Encryption
DetectionRule.Description: Detects encryption of files on network shares, indicating ransomware has spread beyond initial endpoint and is targeting shared resources. Monitors SMB file operations across network shares.
DetectionRule.MITREMapping: T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact, T1210 - Exploitation of Remote Services
DetectionRule.DetectionLogic:
let NetworkSharePaths = dynamic(["\\\\*"]); let SuspiciousExtensions = dynamic([".encrypted", ".locked", ".crypto", ".enc"]);
DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(1h) | where ActionType in~ ("FileCreated", "FileModified", "FileRenamed") | where FolderPath has "\\\\" // Network share path or ShareName != "" // SMB share access | extend Extension = tostring(split(FileName, '.')[-1]) | where Extension in~ (SuspiciousExtensions) or PreviousFileName !~ FileName | summarize AffectedFiles = dcount(SHA256), AffectedShares = dcount(ShareName), UniqueDevices = dcount(DeviceId), TotalSize = sum(FileSize) by ShareName, DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName | where AffectedFiles >= 5 | extend AlertLevel = case( AffectedFiles >= 50, "Critical", AffectedFiles >= 20, "High", AffectedFiles >= 10, "Medium", "Low" ) | project-reorder Timestamp, ShareName, DeviceId, AffectedFiles, AlertLevel

RemediationSteps.Step: Block access to affected shares immediately
Identify initial infection point on network
Isolate all devices with access to affected shares
Restore encrypted files from clean backups
Scan all devices sharing the affected network paths
Implement additional monitoring on critical shares

FalsePositiveReduction.Condition: Exclude approved backup and file sync processes
Require minimum 5 files affected per share

DetectionRule.Name:
Ransom Note Creation Dete} Ransom Note Creation Detection Detects creation of ransom notes and related files containing payment demands, typically with extensions like .txt, .html, .hta, or named README, RECOVER, RESTORE. Identifies attacker attempts to communicate payment and recovery instructions. Critical Impact T1486 Critical (99%) 99% T1486 - Data Encrypted for Impact Ryuk, Conti, LockBit, BlackCat, Egregor, Ragnar
let RansomNoteNames = dynamic([ "README", "readme", "RECOVER", "recover", "RESTORE", "restore", "RECOVERY", "recovery", "HELP", "help", "INSTRUCTION", "instruction", "HOW_TO_DECRYPT", "decrypt", "unlock", "DECRYPT", "FILES", "!README", "!RECOVER", "!DECRYPT" ]);
let RansomNoteExtensions = dynamic([ ".txt", ".html", ".htm", ".hta", ".bmp", ".jpg", ".png" ]);
let RansomKeywords = dynamic([ "ransom", "payment", "bitcoin", "btc", "cryptocurrency", "decrypt", "encrypted", "restore", "recover", "unlock", "pay", "contact", "tor", "onion", "monero", "xmr", "wallet", "key", "private" ]);
DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(1h) | where ActionType in~ ("FileCreated", "FileRenamed") | extend Extension = tolower(tostring(split(FileName, '.')[-1])) | extend BaseName = tostring(split(FileName, '.')[0]) | where // Match known ransom note names (BaseName in~ (RansomNoteNames) and Extension in~ (RansomNoteExtensions)) or // Files with ransom-related keywords in name (FileName has_any (RansomKeywords) and Extension in~ (RansomNoteExtensions)) or // Suspicious file creation patterns (Extension in~ (RansomNoteExtensions) and (FileName has "!" or FileName has "#" or FileName has "_LOCK_") and FileSize < 102400) // Typical note size < 100KB | where not(FileName has "recovery") and not(FileName has "Recovery") | extend NoteScore = case( BaseName in~ (RansomNoteNames), 99, FileName has_any (RansomKeywords), 95, true, 90 ) | project-reorder Timestamp, DeviceId, FileName, FolderPath, NoteScore
1 Critical New true true true
PT1H
ITAdministrator* SecurityTeam*
explorer.exe notepad.exe
IMMEDIATE: Isolate all affected devices Do NOT pay the ransom - contact authorities Preserve ransom notes for law enforcement Identify full scope of encryption Initiate full incident response protocol Engage law enforcement and cyber insurance
Exclude IT team recovery documentation Allow legitimate security awareness testing Time-based exclusions for maintenance Review for penetration test activities
